questions.question_text: A Maine licensee posts a property listing on social media without displaying the agency trade name. Is this compliant?
questions.correct_answer: No, trade name must be prominent
distractors: Only if the agent's name appears
Only if posted after business hours
questions.explanation: Maine requires the agency trade name to be prominently displayed in all advertising, including social media posts. No advertising by a licensee is compliant unless the brokerage's trade name is prominently featured. This applies to all media formats without exception.

questions.question_text: What is the trade name display requirement for Maine real estate advertising on the internet?
distractors: Only required on the main homepage
Not required for any internet ads
Displayed only within the page footer
questions.explanation: For internet advertising, Maine requires the agency trade name to either prominently appear on the advertisement or be readily accessible (such as through a single click). This flexible standard accommodates various digital formats while ensuring consumers can identify the brokerage.

questions.question_text: An agent wants to advertise another brokerage's exclusive listing. What is required?
questions.correct_answer: Prior written consent of the listing DB
distractors: Notification to the real estate board
No permission if the MLS is credited
questions.explanation: Maine prohibits licensees from advertising exclusive listings of other agencies without the prior written consent of the listing designated broker. This protects the listing brokerage's contractual rights and ensures advertising accuracy.

questions.question_text: Who must authorize all advertising by licensees in a Maine brokerage?
questions.explanation: The designated broker must authorize all advertising conducted by affiliated licensees. This ensures that advertising is truthful, free from deception, and properly displays the agency trade name. The DB bears responsibility for advertising compliance across the entire brokerage.

questions.question_text: A licensee exaggerates a property's ocean view in a listing description. Which rule does this violate?
questions.explanation: Maine advertising rules require all advertising to be free from deception. Licensees cannot misrepresent property characteristics, terms, or policies. Exaggerating a property feature like an ocean view constitutes misrepresentation and violates Maine's advertising compliance requirements.

questions.question_text: A group of licensees in a Maine brokerage wants to form a team. Who must authorize the team?
distractors: A majority vote of team members
questions.explanation: In Maine, the designated broker must authorize the formation and operation of any team within the brokerage. The DB retains supervisory responsibility over team activities, advertising, and compliance with all real estate regulations.

questions.question_text: An authorized team uses its team name in print advertising. Under Maine rules, what is the team name legally classified as?
questions.correct_answer: A service mark owned by the agency
distractors: A registered trademark of the team
A personal brand of the team leader
questions.explanation: In Maine, a team name functions as a service mark owned by the brokerage agency, not by the individual team members. This means the team name belongs to the brokerage and cannot be taken to another brokerage without the designated broker's consent.

questions.question_text: Two licensees form an LLC for tax purposes and want to use the LLC name as their team name in advertising. Is this permitted?
distractors: Yes, if the LLC is registered
questions.explanation: Maine prohibits using a business entity name formed for tax purposes as a team name in advertising. Doing so would constitute an unlicensed entity appearing to offer brokerage services, which violates licensing law. The team name must be a service mark under the brokerage.

questions.question_text: A Maine team places an ad with only the team name and no brokerage information. Is this compliant?
questions.correct_answer: No, the agency trade name is required
distractors: Yes, if the team is DB-authorized
Only if the ad runs less than a week
questions.explanation: All Maine real estate advertising, including team advertising, must prominently display the agency trade name. A team name alone is insufficient. The brokerage identity must be clear to consumers regardless of whether a team name is also used.

questions.question_text: A team member leaves the brokerage. Can she continue using the team name at her new agency?
questions.correct_answer: No, the name belongs to the agency
distractors: Yes, if she was the team leader
Only with written team member consent
questions.explanation: Since the team name is a service mark owned by the agency, a departing licensee cannot take the team name to a new brokerage. The designated broker of the original agency retains ownership of the team name. The departing agent would need to establish a new team name at the new brokerage.

questions.question_text: A Maine designated broker discovers an affiliated licensee created a personal website advertising properties without approval. What is the DB's obligation?
questions.correct_answer: Ensure all licensee websites are authorized
distractors: Report the licensee to the state
Ignore it if listings are accurate
questions.explanation: Maine designated brokers are responsible for authorizing all websites used by affiliated licensees for real estate advertising. The DB must review and approve any personal or team websites to ensure they comply with advertising rules, display the trade name, and contain no misrepresentations.

questions.question_text: Where must a Maine designated broker maintain trust account records?
questions.explanation: Maine requires designated brokers to maintain trust account records at the registered business address. This ensures records are accessible for audits and regulatory review. The DB must keep these records organized and available as part of their supervisory responsibilities.

questions.question_text: A new associate broker in Maine asks who oversees her daily activities. Who holds this responsibility?
questions.correct_answer: The designated broker of her agency
distractors: The Maine Real Estate Commission directly
Her most senior team member at the office
questions.explanation: The designated broker is responsible for supervising all affiliated licensees in the brokerage, including new associate brokers. This supervision covers transaction conduct, advertising, trust account handling, and compliance with Maine real estate law and commission rules.

questions.question_text: An affiliated licensee violates advertising rules. Who does the Commission hold primarily accountable?
distractors: The seller who approved the ad
questions.explanation: The designated broker bears primary responsibility for all agency operations, including advertising compliance. While the individual licensee may also face consequences, the DB is held accountable for failing to supervise and ensure that all advertising by affiliated licensees meets Maine requirements.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is a designated broker supervisory duty in Maine?
questions.explanation: Among the designated broker's supervisory duties in Maine are ensuring advertising compliance, supervising affiliated licensees, maintaining trust account records, and authorizing websites. Setting commission rates for individual agents, approving mortgages, and conducting inspections are not DB supervisory duties.
